# python-json-patch

{{ PyPI version }} {{ Supported Python versions }} {{ Build Status }} {{ Coverage Status }}

## Applying JSON Patches in Python

Library to apply JSON Patches according to [RFC
6902](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6902)

See source code for examples

- Website: <https://github.com/stefankoegl/python-json-patch>
- Repository: <https://github.com/stefankoegl/python-json-patch.git>
- Documentation: <https://python-json-patch.readthedocs.org/>
- PyPI: <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/jsonpatch>
- Travis CI: <https://travis-ci.org/stefankoegl/python-json-patch>
- Coveralls: <https://coveralls.io/r/stefankoegl/python-json-patch>

## Running external tests

To run external tests (such as those from
<https://github.com/json-patch/json-patch-tests>) use ext_test.py

```
./ext_tests.py ../json-patch-tests/tests.json
```

functions.name: jsonpatch.multidict
documentation: Convert duplicate keys values to lists.

functions.name: jsonpatch.apply_patch
documentation:
Apply list of patches to specified json document.

```{eval-rst}

:param doc: Document object.
:type doc: dict

:param patch: JSON patch as list of dicts or raw JSON-encoded string.
:type patch: list or str

:param in_place: While :const:`True` patch will modify target document.
                 By default patch will be applied to document copy.
:type in_place: bool

:param pointer_cls: JSON pointer class to use.
:type pointer_cls: Type[JsonPointer]

:return: Patched document object.
:rtype: dict
```

```
>>> doc = {'foo': 'bar'}
>>> patch = [{'op': 'add', 'path': '/baz', 'value': 'qux'}]
>>> other = apply_patch(doc, patch)
>>> doc is not other
True
>>> other == {'foo': 'bar', 'baz': 'qux'}
True
>>> patch = [{'op': 'add', 'path': '/baz', 'value': 'qux'}]
>>> apply_patch(doc, patch, in_place=True) == {'foo': 'bar', 'baz': 'qux'}
True
>>> doc == other
True
```

functions.name: jsonpatch.make_patch
documentation:
Generates patch by comparing two document objects. Actually is
a proxy to {meth}`JsonPatch.from_diff` method.

```{eval-rst}

:param src: Data source document object.
:type src: dict

:param dst: Data source document object.
:type dst: dict

:param pointer_cls: JSON pointer class to use.
:type pointer_cls: Type[JsonPointer]
```

```
>>> src = {'foo': 'bar', 'numbers': [1, 3, 4, 8]}
>>> dst = {'baz': 'qux', 'numbers': [1, 4, 7]}
>>> patch = make_patch(src, dst)
>>> new = patch.apply(src)
>>> new == dst
True
```

classes.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatchException
documentation: Base Json Patch exception

classes.name: jsonpatch.InvalidJsonPatch
documentation: Raised if an invalid JSON Patch is created

classes.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatchConflict
jsonpatch.JsonPatchTestFailed
documentation: A Test operation failed

classes.name: jsonpatch.PatchOperation

methods.name: jsonpatch.PatchOperation.apply
documentation: Abstract method that applies a patch operation to the specified object.
A single operation inside a JSON Patch.

classes.name: jsonpatch.RemoveOperation
documentation: Removes an object property or an array element.

classes.name: jsonpatch.AddOperation
documentation: Adds an object property or an array element.

classes.name: jsonpatch.ReplaceOperation
documentation: Replaces an object property or an array element by a new value.

classes.name: jsonpatch.MoveOperation
documentation: Moves an object property or an array element to a new location.

classes.name: jsonpatch.TestOperation
documentation: Test value by specified location.

classes.name: jsonpatch.CopyOperation
documentation: Copies an object property or an array element to a new location

methods.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatch.__str__
documentation: str(self) -> self.to_string()

methods.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatch.from_string
documentation:
Creates JsonPatch instance from string source.

```{eval-rst}

:param patch_str: JSON patch as raw string.
:type patch_str: str

:param loads: A function of one argument that loads a serialized
              JSON string.
:type loads: function

:param pointer_cls: JSON pointer class to use.
:type pointer_cls: Type[JsonPointer]

:return: :class:`JsonPatch` instance.
```

methods.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatch.from_diff
documentation:
Creates JsonPatch instance based on comparison of two document
objects. Json patch would be created for `src` argument against `dst`
one.

```{eval-rst}

:param src: Data source document object.
:type src: dict

:param dst: Data source document object.
:type dst: dict

:param dumps: A function of one argument that produces a serialized
              JSON string.
:type dumps: function

:param pointer_cls: JSON pointer class to use.
:type pointer_cls: Type[JsonPointer]

:return: :class:`JsonPatch` instance.
```

```
>>> src = {'foo': 'bar', 'numbers': [1, 3, 4, 8]}
>>> dst = {'baz': 'qux', 'numbers': [1, 4, 7]}
>>> patch = JsonPatch.from_diff(src, dst)
>>> new = patch.apply(src)
>>> new == dst
True
```

methods.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatch.to_string
documentation: Returns patch set as JSON string.

methods.name: jsonpatch.JsonPatch.apply
documentation:
Applies the patch to a given object.

:param obj: Document object.
:type obj: dict

:param in_place: Tweaks the way how patch would be applied - directly to
                 specified `obj` or to its copy.
:type in_place: bool

:return: Modified `obj`.
